Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Please merge this test patch into the previous one, since it would be possible that the previous patch could be merged while this test is failing.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> Please merge this test patch into the previous one, since it would be possible that the previous patch could be merged while this test is failing.

Thanks will do, I am not sure but some time back I was asked to split the fix in two part.

Patch Set 1:

> > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
 > >
 > > Please merge this test patch into the previous one, since it
 > would be possible that the previous patch could be merged while
 > this test is failing.
 > 
 > Thanks will do, I am not sure but some time back I was asked to
 > split the fix in two part.

That is an HPE practice, and it makes sense while developing the patch, but it isn't as good to land the patch in two parts afterward.

Optionally, it would be possible for you to change test-framework.sh to label a patch with a keyword in the commit message, and then allow the new subtest to run and "expect FAIL", then later patches it would run normally.  Then the test patch would go first, have a special "one time FAIL allowed" and the fix patch would follow it and the test would need to pass.

That would allow the same HPE development process to be kept, while ensuring the testing is always passing.

Abandoned

will go as part of main commit

I am able to recreate this issue will analyze it and fix it.

parents.subject: LU-18421 utils: Fix Default Stripe Size Creation

commit.subject: LU-18421 test: Verify Default Stripe Size Creation
commit.message:
LU-18421 test: Verify Default Stripe Size Creation

sanity.sh:
Added test 908c to validate the creation of a default
stripe size when no stripe size is explicitly specified.

Test-parameters: sanity
Signed-off-by: Rajeev Mishra <rajeevm@hpe.com>
Change-Id: I19719d5aca6710f123f3ef11b51b3ef6bcf9d143
